codeReview.summary: The fix updates the shared classifier exactly where the prior scaffold review failed: non-tool `item/started` and `item/completed` messages now stay `unknown`, `item/agentMessage/delta` remains the only `agentMessage` path, and the added shared tests cover the regression plus adjacent method families.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture docs still describe the wrapper/server classification vocabulary as `agentMessage`, `toolCall`, `approval`, and `turnLifecycle`, but the shared protocol used by this fix now exposes `serverRequest`, `threadLifecycle`, and `unknown` as first-class categories.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:52 and 107 list `approval` and omit `serverRequest`, `threadLifecycle`, and `unknown`; shared/src/types.ts:9-15 and shared/src/protocol.ts:70-88 define the current message categories.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the mission docs, prior `project-scaffold` failure review, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 8900243cda9deb46cb81be4a18b3e1694259d90f. The change directly fixes the original blocking misclassification by returning `unknown` for non-tool lifecycle items and adds focused shared tests, so the prior failure is adequately addressed with no blocking code issues found.
